message:
Normalize architecture naming in shell installer

Adds cross-platform architecture normalization to ensure consistent artifact
naming when downloading release tarballs across different Unix systems that
report CPU architecture using varying conventions.

Changes:
- Add case statement to map common arch aliases to canonical names
- x86_64 | amd64 → x86_64 (consistent with most Linux distros)
- arm64 | aarch64 → arm64 (matches Apple Silicon and modern ARM naming)

Rationale:
Different systems report architecture differently via `uname -m`:
- Linux x86_64: typically "x86_64"
- Some BSD/Debian derivatives: may report "amd64"
- macOS Apple Silicon: reports "arm64"
- Linux ARM64: typically reports "aarch64"

cargo-dist generates release artifacts with consistent naming (e.g.,
"coding-agent-search-v0.1.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz"), but without normalization
the installer script would fail when uname -m output doesn't exactly match
the artifact naming scheme.

This normalization ensures the installer works reliably across:
- Ubuntu/Debian x86_64 (uname: x86_64)
- Some Debian/BSD systems (uname: amd64)
- Raspberry Pi / ARM servers (uname: aarch64)
- macOS Apple Silicon (uname: arm64)

The normalization happens before URL construction, so all arch-specific
release downloads will succeed regardless of local `uname -m` output
variations.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -26,6 +26,11 @@ done
 mkdir -p "$DEST"
 OS=$(uname -s | tr 'A-Z' 'a-z')
 ARCH=$(uname -m)
+# normalize arch for release names
+case "$ARCH" in
+  x86_64|amd64) ARCH="x86_64" ;;
+  arm64|aarch64) ARCH="arm64" ;;
+esac
 TAR="coding-agent-search-${VERSION}-${OS}-${ARCH}.tar.gz"
 URL="https://github.com/${OWNER}/${REPO}/releases/download/${VERSION}/${TAR}"
